summary: The frontend container follows a component‑based Angular architecture divided into presentation, service/domain, and infrastructure layers, using TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Router, HttpClient and optionally Angular Material to interact with RESTful APIs. It implements a rich set of capabilities around deed and document lifecycle management, signature handling, workflow orchestration, and XNP service integration. While functional coverage is comprehensive, the quality assessment reveals moderate separation, coupling and cohesion with a C grade, highlighting technical debt such as an excess of services, adapters and pipes, and overly heterogeneous modules that increase maintenance complexity.

layers: Infrastructure/Adapter (Adapter services, API modules, Http communication)

capabilities: Create, view, edit, and delete deeds
Handle document import, export, and archiving
Process digital signatures and signature validation
Perform document re‑encryption and security operations
Search, filter and paginate deed and document data
Manage participants, notes, connections and correction notes
Provide authentication, authorization and session handling
Interact with external XNP services for storage, custody, notifications and signatures
Generate reports and metadata for deeds
Display online help and contextual assistance
Handle rate limiting, error handling and busy indicators
integrations: External RESTful APIs for document metadata, job processing, and task management

debt_indicators: Excessive number of services (potential God services)
Large quantity of adapters (possible duplication, missing generic abstraction)
Very high count of pipes (over‑fragmented transformation logic)
Components directly depending on many low‑level services
recommendations: Audit and refactor large services into smaller, focused domain services
Introduce shared abstractions for HTTP communication to reduce the number of adapter services
Consolidate related pipes into reusable utility functions or transform services
Enforce clearer module boundaries (feature‑sliced modules) to improve cohesion
Apply interface‑based injection for adapters to decouple presentation from infrastructure
Implement a service registry or facade layer to limit direct component‑service coupling

overall_assessment: The frontend delivers extensive functional capabilities with a solid Angular foundation, but architectural debt and moderate quality metrics indicate a need for systematic refactoring to improve maintainability and scalability.
top_recommendations: Refactor large services into smaller, domain‑focused services
Create shared HTTP/adapter abstractions to eliminate duplicated adapters
Consolidate fragmented pipes into common utility functions or transform services

layers: Infrastructure/Adapter (Adapter services, API modules, Http communication)
